contentContract.purpose: Read-only presenter guidance for Mission Control scenario narration.
contentContract.agentResponseContent: Forbidden. Capture real portal evidence in the Portal Validation workflow before making diagnosis claims.

promptStageTaxonomy.open-ended: Symptom description only; lets Azure SRE Agent reason freely.
promptStageTaxonomy.direct: Names the failing component; asks for diagnosis.
promptStageTaxonomy.specific: Asks about a particular signal, such as endpoints, memory, events, probes, or resource usage.
promptStageTaxonomy.remediation: Asks how to fix the issue after diagnosis is shown.

catalogRules: Hooks use second-person presenter voice, describe only the failure condition, and stay under 140 characters.
Observe bullets start with a verb, name a Mission Control tile or kubectl output, and are limited to four per scenario.
Suggested prompts are copied exactly from docs/SRE-AGENT-PROMPTS.md when available; otherwise from docs/PROMPTS-GUIDE.md.
Restore labels use Operator Restore or Manual Restore wording; narration never says the agent fixes a scenario.
forbiddenPhrases: You should see the agent say...

scenarios.title: OOMKilled — Meter Service Memory Exhaustion
hook: You have a peak-demand smart meter spike overwhelming meter-service.
observe: Check Mission Control Scenarios tile for one active fault.
Open Mission Control Inventory Matrix for meter-service degraded readiness or restarts.
Run kubectl get pods -n energy -w to watch meter-service restart counts.
Inspect kubectl describe pod -l app=meter-service -n energy for recent container state.

suggestedPrompt.text: Why is the meter-service pod restarting repeatedly?

restorePath.label: Operator Restore: use Mission Control Repair Scenario. Manual Restore: reapply the healthy baseline.

safetyNotes: Azure SRE Agent is GA; this lab pins Microsoft.App/agents@2026-01-01 with the Stable channel, and you should not describe a live diagnosis unless real portal evidence is captured.
Frame remediation as recommendation-first; the operator executes recovery in this demo.

scenarios.title: CrashLoopBackOff — Asset Service Configuration Failure
hook: You have an asset-service rollout exiting repeatedly after an invalid grid configuration.
observe: Check Mission Control Scenarios tile for one active fault.
Open Mission Control Inventory Matrix for asset-service degraded readiness or restarts.
Run kubectl get pods -n energy | grep asset-service to see CrashLoopBackOff.
Inspect kubectl logs -l app=asset-service -n energy --previous for the prior container attempt.

restorePath.label: Operator Restore: use Mission Control Repair Scenario. Manual Restore: reapply the healthy baseline.

safetyNotes: Use observable pod state and logs; do not invent portal wording.
Keep action language operator-controlled unless real approval evidence is available.

scenarios.title: ImagePullBackOff — Dispatch Image Release Issue
hook: You have dispatch-service pods waiting because the release references an image the cluster cannot pull.
observe: Check Mission Control Scenarios tile for one active fault.
Open Mission Control Inventory Matrix for dispatch-service unavailable pods.
Run kubectl get pods -n energy | grep dispatch to see ImagePullBackOff.
Inspect kubectl describe pod -l app=dispatch-service -n energy for image pull events.

suggestedPrompt.text: Why is dispatch-service stuck in ImagePullBackOff?

restorePath.label: Operator Restore: use Mission Control Repair Scenario. Manual Restore: reapply the healthy baseline.

safetyNotes: Discuss image and event signals as investigation clues, not as guaranteed portal phrasing.
Keep the repair path separate from narration; use the existing scenario controls or terminal command.

scenarios.title: High CPU — Grid Frequency Calculation Overload
hook: You have frequency calculation pods consuming excess CPU during an extreme-weather grid event.
observe: Check Mission Control Health heartbeat tile for degraded status.
Open Mission Control Inventory Matrix for workloads affected by contention.
Run kubectl top pods -n energy to compare pod CPU usage.
Run kubectl top nodes to check node pressure.

suggestedPrompt.text: What's the current CPU and memory utilization for each pod in the energy namespace?

restorePath.label: Operator Restore: use Mission Control Repair All if available. Manual Restore: delete the overload deployment.

safetyNotes: Avoid measured time-to-recovery or performance-improvement claims; use live observable signals.
If metrics are unavailable, say the investigation path is prepared rather than claiming a result.

scenarios.title: Pending Pods — Substation Monitor Scheduling Pressure
hook: You have substation-monitor pods stuck Pending because their resource requests exceed cluster capacity.
observe: Check Mission Control Scenarios tile for one active fault.
Open Mission Control Inventory Matrix for substation-monitor unavailable pods.
Run kubectl get pods -n energy | grep substation-monitor to see Pending pods.
Inspect kubectl describe pod -l app=substation-monitor -n energy for scheduling events.

suggestedPrompt.text: Why are the substation-monitor pods stuck in Pending?

restorePath.label: Operator Restore: use Mission Control Repair All if available. Manual Restore: delete the unschedulable workload.

safetyNotes: Do not claim SLO-backed capacity guarantees; this demo tracks availability qualitatively.
Frame scale or right-sizing actions as options for operator review.

scenarios.title: Probe Failure — Grid Health Monitor Misconfiguration
hook: You have grid-health-monitor restarting because its liveness probe points at the wrong endpoint.
observe: Check Mission Control Scenarios tile for one active fault.
Open Mission Control Inventory Matrix for grid-health-monitor restarts.
Run kubectl get pods -n energy -l app=grid-health-monitor -w to watch restarts.
Inspect kubectl describe pod -l app=grid-health-monitor -n energy for probe events.

suggestedPrompt.text: What's wrong with the liveness probe on grid-health-monitor?

restorePath.label: Operator Restore: use Mission Control Repair All if available. Manual Restore: delete the probe-failure workload.

safetyNotes: Use the probe events as live evidence; do not pre-state portal wording.
Keep remediation framed as a recommended configuration review followed by operator action.

scenarios.title: Network Block — Portals and Meter Service Isolated
hook: You have the live portals and meter-service isolated by NetworkPolicies after a security policy update.
observe: Check Mission Control Scenarios tile for one active fault.
Open Mission Control Active Incidents for dependent workload symptoms.
Run kubectl get svc -n energy to get the Grid Dashboard and Ops Console public IPs, then curl both; they should fail while pods remain Running.
Run kubectl get networkpolicy -n energy to inspect policy changes.

suggestedPrompt.text: Are there any network policies blocking the live portals or meter traffic in the energy namespace?

restorePath.label: Operator Restore: use Mission Control Repair All if available. Manual Restore: delete the blocking NetworkPolicies.
restorePath.command: kubectl delete networkpolicy deny-meter-service deny-grid-dashboard deny-ops-console -n energy

safetyNotes: Say the agent diagnoses issues you point it to; do not claim autonomous incident detection.
Keep the network policy repair separate from narration content.

scenarios.title: Missing ConfigMap — Grid Zone Configuration Missing
hook: You have grid-zone-config blocked at startup because its referenced ConfigMap is missing.
observe: Check Mission Control Scenarios tile for one active fault.
Open Mission Control Inventory Matrix for grid-zone-config create errors.
Run kubectl get pods -n energy | grep grid-zone-config to see startup status.
Inspect kubectl describe pod -l app=grid-zone-config -n energy for ConfigMap reference events.

suggestedPrompt.text: What configuration is missing for grid-zone-config?

restorePath.label: Operator Restore: use Mission Control Repair All if available. Manual Restore: delete the config scenario workload.

safetyNotes: Use Kubernetes events as the evidence path; do not fabricate portal wording.
Reference GA status and this lab's API-version pin before discussing Azure SRE Agent behavior in a customer demo.

scenarios.title: MongoDB Down — Meter Database Outage
hook: You have the meter readings database offline while dispatch-service and downstream processing degrade.
observe: Check Mission Control Inventory Matrix for MongoDB unavailable and dispatch-service degraded.
Open Mission Control Active Incidents for cascading workload symptoms.
Run kubectl get deployment mongodb -n energy to compare desired and ready replicas.
Run kubectl get endpoints mongodb -n energy to inspect database endpoint availability.

suggestedPrompt.text: Trace the dependency chain — what broke first and what was impacted downstream?

restorePath.label: Operator Restore: use Mission Control Repair Scenario. Manual Restore: reapply the healthy baseline.

sourceRefs.label: Demo narrative
sourceRefs.section: Scenario 2 — The Climax: MongoDB Down / Cascading Failure

safetyNotes: If the portal is unavailable, complete the manual diagnosis and do not claim a live agent result.
Before saying Azure SRE Agent diagnosed this scenario, record real evidence in Portal Validation.

scenarios.title: Service Mismatch — Meter Service Routing Failure
hook: You have healthy meter-service pods but a Service selector that no longer routes traffic to them.
observe: Check Mission Control Pods tile for healthy pod counts.
Open Mission Control Inventory Matrix for meter-service endpoint symptoms.
Run kubectl get endpoints meter-service -n energy to inspect endpoint availability.
Compare kubectl get svc meter-service -n energy -o jsonpath='{.spec.selector}' with pod labels.

suggestedPrompt.text: Why does the meter-service Service have zero endpoints?

restorePath.label: Operator Restore: use Mission Control Repair Scenario. Manual Restore: reapply the healthy baseline.

sourceRefs.label: Demo narrative
sourceRefs.section: Scenario 3 — The Subtle One: Service Mismatch

safetyNotes: Do not claim the portal identified the selector mismatch until real portal evidence has been captured.
Keep recovery operator-controlled in this demo unless a real approval UI/API is captured.
